title: First Week Priorities: What Can Wait a Little Longer
summary: The first week has its own short list of priorities — distinct from day one, and still lighter than most people expect.
category: After a Loss I: The First Weeks
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: First Week Priorities: What Can Wait a Little Longer | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: The first week has its own short list of priorities — distinct from day one, and still lighter than most people expect.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Funeral/memorial planning and immediate family logistics are the week's real priorities.
Financial and legal notifications can still wait for the first month.
Grief doesn't follow a schedule, and that's normal.

paragraphs: This week's genuine priorities are finalizing funeral or memorial plans, handling immediate logistics (out-of-town family arriving, time off work), and simply getting through the days. Financial institutions, government agencies, and most formal notifications can wait until the following weeks.
It's worth naming directly: grief doesn't pause for a checklist, and having a hard day in the middle of handling logistics is a normal part of this, not a sign you're not coping.

nextSteps: Focus this week on the funeral/memorial and immediate logistics only.
Push formal notifications to the following weeks.
Let grief have room alongside the practical tasks.
disclaimer: This is general pacing guidance — move at whatever speed actually works for your family.
